title: Dungeon Recovery Company procedural recovery job E2E
operationalUse: Validate a seed-based procedural recovery dungeon v1 without claiming Public Release readiness.
userGoal: Generate a deterministic dungeon layout from seed, place three reachable recovery targets, complete all targets, and record Play Mode evidence.

expectedDocuments.expected: Dungeon Recovery Company expands from a fixed first playable into a seed-based procedural recovery job v1.
Generated procedural scripts use a deterministic grid room/corridor generator, BFS reachability validation, and deterministic Play Mode hooks.
Dry-run report lists generated Unity files, scene path, generation rules, reachability validation, fallback behavior, rollback scope, and risks.
Production State Graph includes Procedural Recovery Job feature, requirements, tasks, acceptance criteria, and evidence mapping.
Scene contains a procedural job controller, player, camera, light, UI canvas, and runtime-generated floor/wall/target objects.
Evidence records seeds 1001, 2026, and 7777 with generation, reachability, recovery, random startup seed, and console assertions.

validationChecks.expected: Generated procedural scripts compile with zero compile errors.
Seeds 1001, 2026, and 7777 generate valid connected maps with three reachable targets.
Every seed completes all three recovery targets and reaches Job Complete.
Runtime random seed sampling produces different non-zero seed values across startup generation samples.
Procedural player uses a first-person camera and mouse-look movement controller.
Console error count is zero for the procedural validation run.
Procedural recovery job evidence is exported and linked to REQ-DRC-PROC-* and AC-DRC-PROC-*.

passCriteria: Unity Bridge health endpoint is reachable.
Procedural scene exists and can be opened.
Compile status passes after generated scripts are imported.
Every seed has floor cell count > 0.
Every seed has recovery target count == 3.
Every seed has reachable target count == 3.
Every seed has zero target wall overlaps.
Every seed has zero duplicate target cells.
Every seed reaches Job Complete after deterministic recovery hook.
Different seeds produce different layout signatures.
GenerateWithRandomSeed samples produce different non-zero seed values.
Playable camera is first-person and controlled by mouse-look code.
